architecture.summary: PolySAE extends sparse autoencoders (SAEs) by replacing the linear decoder with a polynomial decoder that models feature interactions through low-rank tensor factorization. The encoder remains linear for interpretability. Information flows as: input activations → linear encoder → sparse coefficients → polynomial decoder (linear terms + pairwise and triple interaction terms via shared projection subspace) → reconstructed activations. Interaction terms are computed via low-rank tensor factorization on a shared projection, adding ~3% parameter overhead compared to standard SAE.
trained_components: polynomial decoder with pairwise and triple interaction terms
low-rank tensor factors for feature interactions

biblio.title: PolySAE: Modeling Feature Interactions in Sparse Autoencoders via Polynomial Decoding

claims.statement: PolySAE achieves an average improvement of ~8% in probing F1 while maintaining comparable reconstruction error across four language models and three SAE variants
PolySAE produces 2–10× larger Wasserstein distances between class-conditional feature distributions compared to standard SAE
Learned interaction weights exhibit negligible correlation with co-occurrence frequency (r = 0.06 vs r = 0.82 for SAE feature covariance)
PolySAE adds ~3% parameter overhead on GPT2
Learned interaction directions causally steer model outputs toward the corresponding compositional semantics
PolySAE captures pairwise and triple feature interactions through low-rank tensor factorization on a shared projection subspace while preserving the linear encoder essential for interpretability
